# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Write Kong configuration file (kong.conf) for production deployment. Set admin_listen to configure Admin API accessibility. Define proxy_listen for client traffic and database connection settings. This is for our Kong Gateway deployment.
# Category: kong_admin_api_exposed

# kong.conf - Kong Gateway Production Configuration

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# General Settings
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Prefix for Kong Nginx configuration files and IPC entities (shm, sem)
# Useful for running multiple Kong instances on the same machine.
prefix = /usr/local/kong/

# Log level for Kong. Set to 'info' or 'warn' for production.
log_level = info

# Disable anonymous usage reporting for production.
anonymous_reports = off

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Database Settings
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# The database used by Kong. Accepted values are 'postgres' or 'cassandra'.
database = postgres

# PostgreSQL connection settings
pg_host = kong-database.internal
pg_port = 5432
pg_user = kong
pg_password = ${KONG_PG_PASSWORD} # Use environment variable for production
# pg_password = <your_strong_password> # Uncomment and replace for testing if not using env vars
pg_database = kong
# pg_ssl = on
# pg_ssl_verify = on
# pg_ssl_cert = /etc/kong/certs/client.pem
# pg_ssl_key = /etc/kong/certs/client.key
# pg_ssl_ca_cert = /etc/kong/certs/ca.pem
# pg_timeout = 5000 # In milliseconds

# Cassandra connection settings (uncomment if using Cassandra)
# cassandra_contact_points = kong-cassandra.internal
# cassandra_port = 9042
# cassandra_user = kong
# cassandra_password = ${KONG_CASSANDRA_PASSWORD} # Use environment variable for production
# cassandra_keyspace = kong
# cassandra_data_centers = us-east-1
# cassandra_ssl = on
# cassandra_ssl_verify = on
# cassandra_ssl_cert = /etc/kong/certs/client.pem
# cassandra_ssl_key = /etc/kong/certs/client.key
# cassandra_ssl_ca_cert = /etc/kong/certs/ca.pem
# cassandra_timeout = 5000 # In milliseconds
# cassandra_consistency = ONE # ONE, LOCAL_QUORUM, QUORUM, etc.
# cassandra_lb_policy = DCAwareRoundRobinPolicy # DCAwareRoundRobinPolicy, RoundRobinPolicy

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Admin API Settings
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Listen address for the Kong Admin API.
# For production, restrict to localhost or an internal network interface.
# Example: Admin API on localhost only
admin_listen = 127.0.0.1:8001, 127.0.0.1:8444 ssl

# Admin API logging (defaults to stdout/stderr if not specified)
admin_access_log = /dev/stdout
admin_error_log = /dev/stderr

# If behind a trusted proxy, list trusted IP addresses or CIDRs.
# trusted_ips = 10.0.0.0/8,192.168.0.0/16

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Proxy (Data Plane) Settings
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Listen address for the Kong Proxy.
# For production, expose to 0.0.0.0 for client traffic.
proxy_listen = 0.0.0.0:8000, 0.0.0.0:8443 ssl

# SSL Certificate and Key for the default server (fallback if no SNI match).
# IMPORTANT: Replace with your actual paths.
ssl_certificate = /etc/kong/certs/kong-default.pem
ssl_certificate_key = /etc/kong/certs/kong-default.key

# Default cipher suite for SSL/TLS connections. Use a secure suite for production.
ssl_cipher_suite = modern
# ssl_cipher_suite = ECDHE-ECDSA-AES128-GCM-SHA256:ECDHE-RSA-AES128-GCM-SHA256:ECDHE-ECDSA-AES256-GCM-SHA384:ECDHE-RSA-AES256-GCM-SHA384:ECDHE-ECDSA-CHACHA20-POLY1305:ECDHE-RSA-CHACHA20-POLY1305:DHE-RSA-AES128-GCM-SHA256:DHE-RSA-AES256-GCM-SHA384

# SSL/TLS protocols to enable. Disable older, insecure protocols.
ssl_protocols = TLSv1.2 TLSv1.3

# Proxy API logging (defaults to stdout/stderr if not specified)
proxy_access_log = /dev/stdout
proxy_error_log = /dev/stderr

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Performance Settings
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Number of worker processes. 'auto' uses as many as CPU cores.
nginx_worker_processes = auto

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Developer Settings (Optional for Production)
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# If you have custom plugins, specify the directory here.
# plugins = bundled,my-custom-plugin
# plugins_directory = /etc/kong/plugins/
